title: feat(ltx-2): i2v — gateway downloads image, backend takes base64
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
Alternative to #10953 — same LTX-2 i2v capability, but image download stays on the gateway like every other model.

## Changes
- Gateway: download init image with shared `downloadUserImage` (used by klein/wan/qwen/seedance/veo), send `image_b64` + `image_mime` to `/enqueue`
- GH200 wrapper: accept base64, write to ComfyUI input dir, splice `LoadImage` + `LTXVImgToVideo` at the `EmptyLTXVLatentVideo` (177:116) seam, rewire latent + conditioning consumers
- Registry: `ltx-2` `inputModalities` → `["text", "image"]`

## Why
- Single image-fetch boundary on the gateway (auth, SSRF, future safety/tracking hooks)
- No new `urllib` download helper on the GPU box — reuses the existing convention
- Same splice logic as #10953, only the transport changed

## Test plan
- [ ] Deploy gateway + GH200 wrapper
- [ ] `gen.pollinations.ai/image/...?model=ltx-2&image=<url>` end-to-end
- [ ] Verify t2v path (no `image` param) still works

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

gist.headline: LTX-2 learns to see
gist.blurb: The LTX-2 video model now supports image-to-video generation. Just pass an image URL and watch your static pixels come to life.
gist.summary: Added image-to-video support for the LTX-2 model by allowing the gateway to fetch initial images and pass them as base64 to the ComfyUI backend.
gist.impact: Users can now generate videos from starting images using the ltx-2 model by passing an image=<url> parameter to the API.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot (Polly) holding a static photograph, handing it to the Monitor Robot who is turning it into a glowing, moving film strip. Represents image-to-video transformation.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot holding a small static photograph, handing it to a chunky robot with a CRT monitor head. The robot is processing the photo and projecting a glowing film strip out of its screen. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley, chunky sprites, emotionally warm.
